en: I wish I could tell them how grateful they should be to you.
You were every bit as brave as I was. Harriet, you're talking in the wrong direction.
ko: 좋아 듣고 있으면 나랑 비슷해 태도가 좋아

en: Don't worry about Granny. She's getting ready to give Cousin Maud a grilling.
ko: 합체해서 하나의 끔찍한 롭 밀러가 되는 거야

en: Mainly, I suspect, about her maid. I think Lady Bagshaw sees her as more than just her maid.
Oh, I'm sure she does. And that's what Granny's afraid of.
ko: 수술을 하고 싶긴 한데 서로 없이 어떻게 될지 모르겠어

en: I might as well admit, I like her.
Good. Don't let them put you off.
Tom, you're looking pleased with yourself.
ko: 이래서 좋아하는 거구나 왜 마누라가 나랑 결혼했는지 알겠어

en: He was just praising Lady Bagshaw's maid.
ko: 이래서 좋아하는 거구나 왜 마누라가 나랑 결혼했는지 알겠어

en: How did she contrive to make your acquaintance?
She didn't contrive anything. We met by chance.
This is good. I thought something else was planned.
ko: 좋아. 다른 게 계획된 줄 알았어.

en: But it is excellent. So, a well done to old Corbet.
ko: 하지만 정말 훌륭해. 그래서, 오래된 코르베에게 잘했다고 해줘.

en: This wasn't Monsieur Corbet, Your Majesty. Mrs. Patmore cooked it.
ko: 이건 코르베 씨가 아니에요, 폐하. 패트모어 부인이 만들었어요.
